rulings.Case Title: PETER PEDRETTI et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant S.T.M. Automotive, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Prosecution [C.C.P. §§ 583.310 And 583.360]
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 14, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant S.T.M. Automotive, Inc.'s motion to dismiss for lack of prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. §§ 583.310, 583.360 is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless defendant informs Paul Pedretti, Jr., the sole remaining plaintiff, of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Defendant must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CLAYTON FLOYD JR VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs Regarding Defendant Dana Companies, Llc'S (Fka Dana Corporation) Memorandum Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 14, 2015 in Department 503, Line 4. Plaintiffs' motion to tax costs regarding defendant Dana Companies, LLC's memorandum of costs is granted. Defendant's total claimed costs of $4,903.97 are taxed in the amount of $3,038.03. Therefore the total amount of defendant's allowable costs is $1,865.94. Defendant agrees to this reduction. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS W TAYLOR VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Defendant Moore Mccormack Resources, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 14, 2015 in Department 503, Line 5. Defendant Moore McCormack Resources, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. The Certificates of Discharge create a triable issue whether Mr. Taylor was aboard defendant's ship, a fact which defendant does not dispute. The Court agrees with plaintiff that whether Mr. Taylor was aboard defendant's ship was the only issue raised by defendant's undisputed material facts laid out in its Separate Statement. Therefore the Court did not consider any issue other than that. Additionally, the Court treated this motion as solely a motion for summary judgment. Defendant's Notice of Motion and Separate Statement failed to specifically and separately state each cause of action sought to be summarily adjudicated, in violation of CRC 3.1350. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
